summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. The studies specifically supported the distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora* as a separate taxonomic group and led to the formal description of *Amphora terroris* as a new species. By combining DNA sequence data with comparative morphological traits, the research underscores the necessity of a polyphasic approach for accurate classification within this globally distributed group. The geographic scope of the work encompasses diverse marine and estuarine environments, reflecting the broad ecological range of *Amphora* species worldwide.
